provisions.description: Requires the Comptroller General to conduct an audit of and issue a publicly available report on barriers that prevent fire departments from accessing Federal funds and on the United States Fire Administration.
deadlines: three years after the date of enactment
provisions.raw_text: Not later than three years after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct an audit of and issue
notes: GAO audit covers two topics: (1) barriers to fire departments accessing Federal funds, and (2) the United States Fire Administration
Report must be publicly available

provisions.description: Directs the NRC to coordinate all work relating to import/export licensing for nuclear reactors and radioactive materials, international regulatory cooperation, and support interagency/international coordination on technical standards, capacity building, and exchange programs. Authorizes establishment of an 'International Nuclear Export and Innovation Branch' within the Office of International Programs. Requires interagency coordination with Secretary of State, Secretary of Energy, and other agencies. Includes savings clause preserving Commission authority to license and regulate civilian use of radioactive materials.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear energy export and international cooperation
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: The Commission shall—(A) coordinate all work of the Commission relating to—(i) import and export licensing for nuclear reactors and radioactive materials; and (ii
notes: Subsection (a) establishes coordination duties for the NRC Commission
Subsection (b) authorizes (but does not require) creation of the 'International Nuclear Export and Innovation Branch'
Subsection (d) requires coordination with State, Energy, and other agencies
Subsection (e) is a savings clause preserving existing NRC authority
No dollar amounts in this provision

provisions.description: Amends Section 102 of the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act (42 U.S.C. 2215) to require the Commission to identify international nuclear export and innovation activities in its annual budget justification and to exclude costs for those activities from the NRC fee base. Effective October 1, 2025.
provisions.policy_area: NRC fee structure / international nuclear activities

notes: Subsection (c) of SEC. 101

Effective date: October 1, 2025
Effect: international nuclear export and innovation activity costs excluded from NRC licensee fee recovery base

cross_references.description: Adds new paragraph (4) to subsection (a) and new clause (iv) to subsection (b)(1)(B)

provisions.description: Prohibits any person subject to NRC jurisdiction from possessing or owning covered fuel (enriched uranium fabricated outside the U.S. into fuel assemblies by Russian or Chinese government-owned/controlled entities or entities under their jurisdiction) without an NRC license under section 53 of the Atomic Energy Act. Requires NRC to consult with Secretary of Energy and Secretary of State before issuing such a license. Prohibits license issuance if Secretaries of Energy and State jointly determine possession/ownership poses a national security threat, or if no determination is made. Establishes timeline: 30-day notice to Secretaries, 180-day determination window, immediate Commission notification, 30-day congressional notification, and 15-day public notice. Includes savings clause for existing and future treaties.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear fuel supply chain / national security
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: Unless specifically authorized by the Commission in a license issued under section 53 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (42 U.S.C. 2073) and part 70 of
notes: Targets enriched uranium fuel assemblies fabricated outside the U.S. by Russian or Chinese entities
Creates a joint DOE/State Department national security determination process
If no determination is made within 180 days, license cannot be issued (subsection (c)(2)(C))
Savings clause in subsection (d) preserves existing treaties and international agreements

provisions.description: Requires the NRC, after consulting with the Secretary of State and other relevant agencies, to notify appropriate committees of Congress (including Senate Foreign Relations, Senate Energy and Natural Resources, and House Foreign Affairs) when issuing export licenses for transfers of specified nuclear items to countries that have not concluded and ratified an IAEA Additional Protocol or have not ratified/acceded to the amendment to the Convention on the Physical Protection of Nuclear Material. Covered items include unirradiated nuclear fuel containing special nuclear material (excluding LEU), reactors using such fuel, and reprocessing/separation plant components listed in 10 CFR Part 110 Appendix I.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: If the Commission, after consultation with the Secretary of State and any other relevant agencies, issues an export license for the transfer of any item
notes: Low-enriched uranium (< 20% U-235) is excluded from the notification requirement
Notification triggered by two country criteria: lack of IAEA Additional Protocol or lack of ratification of CPPNM amendment
Items covered include fuel with special nuclear material, reactors, and reprocessing/separation components

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Energy, in consultation with the Secretary of State, Secretary of Commerce, EPA Administrator, and NRC, to conduct a study within 1 year of enactment on the global status of the civilian nuclear energy industry and its supply chains. Study must include information on industry status and long-term risks, emission reduction potential, role in U.S. foreign policy, importance to allied countries, collaboration opportunities, competitors' use of nuclear energy in foreign policy, effects of U.S. involvement in international markets and standards, benefits to other U.S. industries, supply chain challenges, and recommendations for national strategy. Report to Congress within 180 days of study completion, including classified annex if necessary.
deadlines: 180 days after study completion (report to Congress)
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Energy, in consultation with the Secretary of State, the Secretary of Co
notes: Comprehensive assessment covering industry status, foreign policy, nonproliferation, supply chain, and strategic competition
Must include recommendations for national strategy to increase nuclear energy's role in diplomacy
Classified annex permitted if necessary

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Energy, with the concurrence of the Secretary of State, to identify and evaluate factors (beyond Section 123 agreements) within 90 days of enactment that may determine a country's generally authorized destination status under 10 CFR Part 810. Requires the Secretary to review and update the DOE's process for making such determinations incorporating the identified factors. Requires review and amendment of the Appendix A list within 1 year of enactment, and at least once every 5 years thereafter.
deadlines: At least once every 5 years (subsequent reviews)

provisions.raw_text: Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Energy, with the concurrence of the Secretary of State, shall identify

provisions.description: Amends the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act to reform NRC fee structures for advanced nuclear reactor applicants and pre-applicants. Adds definitions for 'advanced nuclear reactor applicant', 'advanced nuclear reactor pre-applicant', 'agency support', 'hourly rate for mission-direct program salaries and benefits', 'mission-direct program salaries and benefits', and 'mission-indirect program support'. Caps the hourly rate charged to advanced nuclear reactor applicants and pre-applicants at the hourly rate for mission-direct program salaries and benefits, excluding mission-indirect program support and agency support costs. Includes a sunset for pre-applicant fee provisions on September 30, 2030, and an effective date of October 1, 2025.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear energy regulation / NRC fee reform
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 201. 
Fees for advanced nuclear reactor application review
(a) 
Definitions
Section 3 of the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act (42 U.S.C. 2215 note; Publ
notes: Amends Section 3, Section 102(b)(1)(B), Section 102(b)(2), and adds Section 102(g) of the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act (P.L. 115-439)
Caps hourly fee rate for advanced nuclear reactor applicants and pre-applicants to mission-direct program salaries and benefits only, excluding overhead costs
Subsection (d) sunsets the pre-applicant fee provisions (paragraphs (1)(B)(vi) and (2)(C) of subsection (b)) on September 30, 2030
Subsection (e) sets effective date of all amendments at October 1, 2025
No appropriation or dollar amount is specified; this is a fee structure policy change

provisions.description: Authorizes the Secretary of Energy to award prizes to the first eligible entities (non-Federal entities and TVA) to receive NRC operating licenses or combined license findings for advanced nuclear reactors in five award categories. Award amounts equal the total NRC fees assessed and collected from the entity for licensing costs. Categories include: (i) first advanced nuclear reactor licensed; (ii) first using spent fuel/depleted uranium as fuel; (iii) first nuclear integrated energy system; (iv) first flexible operation reactor for nonelectric applications; (v) first licensed under the technology-inclusive regulatory framework. Federal funding limitations apply, excluding TVA power program funds and requiring deduction of federal funds received for the project. Awards need not be repaid.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear energy / advanced reactor licensing incentives

Advanced nuclear reactor prizes
Section 103 of the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act (Public Law 115–439; 132 Stat. 5571) is amended by addin

Notwithstanding section 169 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (42 U.S.C. 2209) — overrides standard NRC fee provisions
Award amount is variable: equals total NRC fees collected from the awardee for the licensed project, minus federal cost-share
Five distinct award categories for first-of-a-kind advanced reactors
TVA power program funds are excluded from the definition of 'Federal funds' for the funding limitation
Notwithstanding 31 U.S.C. 9104(a)(4) — awardees are not required to repay or pay dividends

provisions.description: Requires the NRC to submit a report to Congress within 270 days of enactment addressing unique licensing issues or requirements relating to: (1) flexible operation of advanced nuclear reactors; (2) exclusive use of advanced nuclear reactors for nonelectric applications; and (3) colocation of nuclear reactors with industrial plants or other facilities. The report must address applications including hydrogen production, water desalination, industrial process heat, district heating, energy storage, isotope production, and others. Must include cost estimates, proposed budgets, and timeframes for implementation.

Report must describe options for addressing issues within existing regulatory framework, the technology-inclusive framework under NEIMA Section 103(a)(4), or through new rulemaking

provisions.description: Amends Section 11 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to define the term 'fusion machine' as a machine capable of transforming atomic nuclei through fusion processes and capturing the resultant products; modifies the definition of byproduct material to include material made radioactive by use of a fusion machine; and redesignates and reorders subsections.

notes: Subsection (a) amends the Atomic Energy Act to establish 'fusion machine' as a defined term
Modifies 'byproduct material' definition to include fusion machine products
Redesignates and reorders subsections ee. through jj. of section 11
This is the core statutory change establishing a regulatory framework for fusion energy

provisions.description: Requires the Commission to establish and carry out an expedited combined license review procedure under section 185 b. of the Atomic Energy Act. Applicants must reference a certified design or a substantially similar design and propose to build on or adjacent to an existing/former licensed commercial reactor site. Sets deadlines of 18 months for technical review and environmental review, 2 years for public hearings, and 25 months for a final licensing decision after docketing.

notes: Subsections (a) through (c) of SEC. 207

Applicant may not be subject to suspension/revocation order
Timelines are 'to the maximum extent practicable'

provisions.description: Requires the Commission within 18 months to develop risk-informed and performance-based strategies and guidance to license and regulate micro-reactors, covering staffing, operations, oversight, inspections, safeguards, security, emergency preparedness, risk analysis methods, decommissioning funding assurance, transportation of fueled micro-reactors, and siting (including population density, mobile deployment, and environmental reviews). Requires implementation within 3 years through existing framework, technology-inclusive framework under NEIMA, or rulemaking. Commission must consult with the Secretary of Energy, other Federal agencies, micro-reactor developers, and other stakeholders.

Implementation may occur through existing framework, NEIMA section 103(a)(4) technology-inclusive framework, or new rulemaking

provisions.description: Exempts entities owned, controlled, or dominated by OECD member countries (excluding sanctioned countries) or the Republic of India from the Atomic Energy Act's prohibitions on issuing utilization facility licenses to aliens, foreign corporations, or foreign-dominated entities, provided the Commission determines issuance is not inimical to common defense and security or public health and safety.

provisions.raw_text: Section 103 d. of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (42 U.S.C. 2133(d)) is amended, the second sentence, by striking ''any any'' and inserting ''any''.
notes: Subsection (c) of SEC. 301
Corrects a duplicated word in existing law

cross_references.target: Section 103(d) of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (42 U.S.C. 2133(d))
cross_references.description: Technical correction striking duplicated 'any'

provisions.description: Savings clause preserving the requirements of section 721 of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (CFIUS review authority) notwithstanding the foreign ownership exemptions created by this section.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear energy / National security review (CFIUS)
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: Nothing in this section affects the requirements of section 721 of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C. 4565).
notes: Subsection (d) of SEC. 301
Ensures CFIUS review authority under the Defense Production Act is not affected by the foreign ownership exemption

cross_references.target: Section 721 of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C. 4565)
cross_references.description: CFIUS review requirements preserved

provisions.description: Requires the Commission (NRC) to submit a report to Congress within 180 days on manufacturing and construction for nuclear energy projects, covering advanced manufacturing processes, advanced construction techniques, iterative innovation, nuclear-grade component requirements, transport of fueled advanced reactor cores, safety aspects not addressed by existing codes and standards, and proposed budgets and timeframes for implementing risk-informed regulatory guidance.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Commission shall submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report (referred
notes: Report must include stakeholder input from DOE, industry, National Labs, universities, unions, NGOs, and others
Report must include cost estimates, proposed budgets, and proposed timeframes for implementing regulatory guidance
Commission refers to the Nuclear Regulatory Commission

provisions.description: Amends Section 313 of division C of the Omnibus Appropriations Act, 2009 to establish a nuclear energy traineeship subprogram under the NRC, in coordination with institutions of higher education and trade schools, to competitively award traineeships for critical mission needs of the Commission and nuclear workforce needs, including nuclear tradecraft workforce needs.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear energy workforce development and training
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: Section 313 of division C of the Omnibus Appropriations Act, 2009 (42 U.S.C. 16274a), is amended—(1) subsection (a), by striking ''Nuclear Regulatory''
notes: Amends 42 U.S.C. 16274a to broaden the existing program and add a nuclear energy traineeship subprogram
Requires coordination with Secretary of Energy to prioritize traineeships focusing on nuclear workforce needs and critical NRC mission needs
Requires annual evaluation of nuclear workforce needs
Encourages partnerships among National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, trade schools, and industry

cross_references.target: Section 313 of division C of the Omnibus Appropriations Act, 2009 (42 U.S.C. 16274a)
cross_references.description: Amends existing nuclear science and engineering support program to add traineeship subprogram

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Energy to submit biennial reports to Congress beginning January 1, 2026, describing payments made due to partial breach of standard contracts under the Nuclear Waste Policy Act, cumulative DOE spending on spent nuclear fuel and high-level radioactive waste storage and management, projected lifecycle costs, mechanisms for better accounting of liabilities, recommendations for improving cost accounting, interim storage actions, and activities to develop and deploy technologies enhancing safe transportation or storage of spent nuclear fuel or high-level radioactive waste.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: Not later than January 1, 2026, and biennially thereafter, the Secretary of Energy shall submit to Congress a report that describes—(1) the annual and c
notes: Report covers 8 categories of information related to spent nuclear fuel and high-level radioactive waste
Includes projected lifecycle costs through 2050 for existing reactor inventory
References standard contracts under the Nuclear Waste Policy Act of 1982
Covers cumulative DOE spending since fiscal year 2008

cross_references.target: Nuclear Waste Policy Act of 1982 (42 U.S.C. 10101 et seq.)
cross_references.description: Defines terms and context for standard contracts and partial breach of contract
Definition of standard contract

provisions.description: Requires the Commission (NRC) to establish an initiative to enhance preparedness and coordination on qualification and licensing of advanced nuclear fuel, and requires the Commission and the Secretary of Energy to enter into a memorandum of understanding within 180 days to share technical expertise on accident tolerant fuels, advanced reactor fuel concepts, and related matters. Also requires a report to Congress within 2 years describing the Commission's efforts, including assessments of preparedness to review advanced fuel types (accident tolerant fuel, ceramic cladding, silicon carbide fuels, HALEU fuels, molten-salt liquid fuels, fuels from spent fuel or depleted uranium).
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: The Commission shall establish an initiative to enhance preparedness and coordination with respect to the qualification and licensing of advanced nuclear
notes: MOU must cover sharing technical expertise, enabling testing/demonstration of accident tolerant fuels, operating a shared database, and leveraging safety analysis expertise
MOU must ensure DOE has sufficient expertise for R&D and NRC has sufficient expertise for licensing evaluations
Report must include assessment of NRC preparedness to review 7 categories of advanced fuel concepts
Requires consultation with DOE, National Labs, industry, technology developers, NGOs, and public stakeholders for the report

provisions.description: Requires the NRC to update its mission statement within 1 year of enactment to include that licensing and regulation of civilian use of radioactive materials and nuclear energy be conducted efficiently and not unnecessarily limit civilian use or its benefits to society, and to submit a report to Congress describing the updated mission statement and guidance to staff.
deadlines: on completion of mission statement update
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Commission shall, while remaining consistent with the policies of the Atomic Energy
notes: Subsection (a) directs the mission statement update; subsection (b) requires a report to appropriate committees of Congress describing the updated mission statement and guidance to staff

Must remain consistent with Atomic Energy Act of 1954 and Energy Reorganization Act of 1974 policies including adequate protection of public health and safety, common defense and security, and environmental protection

cross_references.target: Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (42 U.S.C. 2011 et seq.)
cross_references.description: Mission update must remain consistent with policies of this Act

cross_references.target: Energy Reorganization Act of 1974 (42 U.S.C. 5801 et seq.)
cross_references.description: Mission update must remain consistent with policies of this Act

provisions.description: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 by inserting new Section 161B granting the NRC Chairman direct hire authority for up to 210 exceptionally well-qualified individuals in covered positions and up to 20 term-limited appointments per fiscal year (terms not exceeding 4 years), authority to fix compensation without regard to GS pay rates (capped at Executive Schedule Level III), authority to pay one-time hiring bonuses up to $25,000 and performance bonuses up to $25,000 (with 5-year cooldown), and related reporting, delegation, and sunset provisions.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear Regulatory Commission workforce and hiring authorities
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: The Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (42 U.S.C. 2011 et seq.) is amended by inserting after section 161A the following: SEC. 161B. Commission workforce
notes: Creates new Section 161B of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954
Direct hire authority: up to 210 covered positions at any time under (a)(1)(A); up to 20 term-limited positions per fiscal year under (a)(1)(B)
Term-limited positions may not exceed 4 years
Compensation capped at Executive Schedule Level III; excludes Senior Executive Service positions
One-time hiring bonus up to $25,000 for new hires; performance bonus up to $25,000 with 5-year cooldown between performance bonuses
Exceptional performance includes timely licensing reviews, resolution of novel regulatory issues, improving Commission effectiveness
Chairman must develop implementation plan within 180 days and report to House Energy and Commerce, Senate EPW, and OPM before implementing
Budget materials starting FY2026 must include hiring, vacancy, and compensation information

Covered positions: highly-specialized scientific/technical/engineering/mathematical positions for critical licensing or regulatory oversight needs, or positions determined necessary by the Executive Director for Operations
Authorities sunset September 30, 2034, extendable to September 30, 2039 if Commission certifies continued necessity
Notwithstanding section 161 d. of the Atomic Energy Act and Reorganization Plan No. 1 of 1980

cross_references.target: Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (42 U.S.C. 2011 et seq.)
cross_references.description: Inserts new Section 161B after Section 161A

cross_references.target: Section 161 d. of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954
cross_references.description: Direct hire authority overrides this provision

cross_references.description: Direct hire authority overrides this provision

Aggregate limitation on pay applicable to performance bonus cap

provisions.description: Requires the Comptroller General to submit a report to House Energy and Commerce, House Oversight and Accountability, Senate EPW, and Senate HSGAC evaluating the utilization and effectiveness of the Section 161B workforce authorities, assessing their role in licensing advanced nuclear reactors, and making recommendations on strategic workforce management and whether to extend, enhance, modify, or discontinue the authorities.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Not later than September 30, 2033, the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee
notes: This is subsection (b) of SEC. 502

GAO report must cover: (1) utilization of authorities, (2) role of appointees in advanced nuclear reactor licensing, (3) effectiveness assessment, (4) strategic workforce management recommendations, (5) recommendations on extending/modifying/discontinuing authorities

cross_references.target: Section 161B of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954
cross_references.description: Evaluates the authorities created by this section

provisions.description: Requires the Commission to annually solicit applications for the Nuclear Regulator Apprenticeship Network.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: The Commission, on an annual basis, shall solicit applications for the Nuclear Regulator Apprenticeship Network.
notes: This is subsection (c) of SEC. 502
NRAN is the Nuclear Regulator Apprenticeship Network

provisions.description: Requires the Commission to submit to the appropriate committees of Congress and make publicly available a report within 3 years describing progress on implementation of corporate support budget authority caps under section 102(a)(3) of the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act and whether the Commission is meeting those caps.

provisions.policy_area: Nuclear Regulatory Commission corporate support cost limitation

notes: Subsection (b) of SEC. 503; sets a permanent 30% cap on NRC corporate support costs starting FY2025

provisions.description: Amends paragraph (10) of section 3 of the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act to clarify that 'corporate support costs' excludes: (i) costs for rent and utilities for space in Three White Flint North building not occupied by the Commission; and (ii) costs for salaries, travel, and other support for the Office of the Commission.

notes: Subsection (c) of SEC. 503; narrows the definition of corporate support costs by excluding certain rent/utilities and Office of the Commission costs

provisions.description: Amends Section 102(c) of the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act to shorten the deadline in paragraph (3) from 180 days to 90 days and adds a new paragraph (4) requiring the Commission to review and assess performance metrics and milestone schedules not less than every 3 years and revise them as appropriate.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear Regulatory Commission performance metrics and milestone schedules

provisions.raw_text: Section 102(c) of the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act (42 U.S.C. 2215(c)) is amended—(1) in� paragraph (3)—(A) on the paragraph head
notes: Reduces reporting deadline from 180 to 90 days and adds triennial review/revision requirement for performance metrics

provisions.description: Amends Section 203 of the Energy Reorganization Act of 1974 to add a new subsection (c) directing the Director of Nuclear Reactor Regulation to establish and maintain techniques and guidance for evaluating license applications to support efficient, timely, and predictable reviews, subject to Commission approval for policy formulation.

provisions.description: Amends Section 181 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to add a new subsection (b) requiring the Commission to provide for efficient and timely reviews and proceedings for granting, suspending, revoking, or amending licenses, construction permits, or transfer control applications.

provisions.description: Amends Section 185 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to add a new subsection c requiring the Commission, when reviewing an application for an early site permit, construction permit, operating license, or combined license for a facility at an existing licensed site, to use, to the extent practicable, information that was part of the licensing basis of the previously licensed facility.
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear licensing at existing sites

provisions.description: Requires the Commission to submit a report to Congress within 180 days on efforts to facilitate efficient, timely, and predictable environmental reviews of nuclear reactor license applications, including through expanded use of categorical exclusions, environmental assessments, and generic environmental impact statements. The report must describe implementation of NEPA amendments from the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023, consider numerous streamlining measures (adoption of other agencies' environmental documents, use of mitigated FONSIs, coordination with other agencies, use of applicant EIS as draft, digital technologies, etc.), and include a schedule for any necessary rulemakings.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Commission shall submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report on the ef
notes: Extensive directive with 12 sub-considerations (A through L) for streamlining NRC environmental reviews under NEPA

provisions.description: Requires the Commission within 1 year to develop and submit a report identifying specific improvements to nuclear reactor and materials oversight and inspection programs to maximize efficiency through risk-informed, performance-based procedures, expanded use of information technologies, and staff training. Report must cover assessment of oversight elements (travel, planning, document collection, cross-cutting issues), measures to reduce duplication, risk-informed procedures, enabling licensee innovation, and an implementation timeline. Requires stakeholder input from other federal agencies, industry, NGOs, and public.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Commission shall develop and submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report
notes: Comprehensive inspection/oversight modernization directive with 7 major report elements covering technology, risk-informed approaches, AI/ML, and licensee innovation

provisions.description: Amends Section 104 c. of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to restructure and add limitations on NRC licensing of utilization facilities for research and development activities, including caps on annual costs devoted to sale of nonenergy services and energy (75% combined, 50% for energy alone).
provisions.policy_area: Nuclear energy regulation
provisions.title: VI
provisions.raw_text: Section 104 c. of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (42 U.S.C. 2134(c)) is amended—(1) by striking the third sentence and inserting the following:(3)
notes: Restructures Section 104(c) into numbered paragraphs with headings
Establishes two-pronged cost limitation: not more than 75% of annual costs for sale of nonenergy services and/or energy, and not more than 50% for sale of energy alone
Section heading indicates this is a 'Technical correction'

cross_references.description: Restructures and adds limitations on utilization facility licensing for R&D activities

provisions.description: Requires the Commission (NRC) to submit to appropriate congressional committees a report describing any engagement between the Commission and the Government of Canada with respect to nuclear waste issues in the Great Lakes Basin.
deadlines: 1 year after the date of enactment

provisions.description: Savings clause providing that nothing in this Act affects authorities of the Department of State.
provisions.policy_area: Preservation of existing authority
provisions.title: VI
provisions.raw_text: Nothing in this Act affects authorities of the Department of State.
notes: Standard savings clause preserving Department of State authorities, likely related to the international engagement provisions elsewhere in the Act

flagged_issues: This is an authorization bill, not an appropriations bill; all dollar amounts are authorizations of appropriations, not direct budget authority
SEC. 4(b) does not state an explicit dollar amount—the authorization level ($750,000,000) is implicit from the existing subparagraph (H) of section 34(j)(1) being extended to FY2024-2028
Bill identifier assumed to be S. 870 based on user reference to '870' and the short title 'Fire Grants and Safety Act of 2023'
This is an authorization title with no appropriations — all provisions are policy riders or directives
SEC. 101(c) amends NEIMA to exclude international activities from NRC fee base, effective October 1, 2025
SEC. 102 creates a de facto ban on Russian/Chinese-fabricated nuclear fuel absent joint DOE/State determination
Bill preamble was empty; identifier inferred as S. 870 from internal reference to 'ADVANCE Act of 2024'
Title II contains no appropriations — all provisions are authorization/policy changes and a reporting requirement
SEC. 202 authorizes prizes 'subject to the availability of appropriations' but no funds are appropriated in this title
Bill identifier '870' provided by user without chamber prefix (H.R. or S.) or Congress number
No dollar amounts or appropriations in Division B, Title II — all provisions are regulatory/policy directives and riders related to NRC nuclear energy licensing
SEC. 204 references section 201(b) which appears earlier in the same bill but is not included in this excerpt
SEC. 205(b)(2) references section 201(a) for prior amendment to the same section

Title III contains no appropriations or spending provisions; all provisions are policy riders amending the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to allow foreign ownership of nuclear facilities by allied-nation entities
Title IV contains no appropriations or dollar amounts — all provisions are authorizing/directive in nature
SEC. 402 amends existing law (42 U.S.C. 16274a) to create a new traineeship subprogram but does not authorize specific funding levels
Title V is entirely authorizing/policy language with no appropriations. SEC. 502(a) creates significant new NRC hiring and compensation authorities via amendment to the Atomic Energy Act of 1954.
Workforce authorities sunset September 30, 2034, extendable to September 30, 2039 upon Commission certification.
Title V contains no appropriations or dollar amounts; all provisions are policy riders or reporting directives related to Nuclear Regulatory Commission reform
Bill identifier '870' was provided without prefix (H.R./S.) — exact bill number uncertain
Title VI contains no spending provisions; all sections are policy riders or directives
Bill identifier inferred as S. 870 based on content (ADVANCE Act) and user instruction
